themes.description: The absolute oneness and uniqueness of Allah — the foundation of Islamic faith

ayahs.key_phrase: Say: He is Allah, the One
Your God is one God; there is no deity except Him
That is Allah, your Lord; there is no deity except Him, the Creator of all things
Had there been gods besides Allah, both heavens and earth would have been ruined
He is Allah, other than whom there is no deity — the Sovereign, the Pure

themes.description: Enduring hardship with faith, trusting Allah's plan

ayahs.key_phrase: O you who believe, seek help through patience and prayer
We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger
Be patient, endure, remain stationed
The patient will be given their reward without account
Indeed, with hardship comes ease
And advised each other to patience

themes.description: Being thankful to Allah for His blessings and recognizing His favors

ayahs.key_phrase: If you are grateful, I will surely increase you
Remember Me; I will remember you. Be grateful to Me and do not deny Me
Be grateful to Allah. Whoever is grateful is grateful for his own soul
So which of the favors of your Lord would you deny?
If you should count the favors of Allah, you could not enumerate them

themes.description: Establishing justice in all affairs — personal, social, and legal

ayahs.key_phrase: Be persistently standing firm in justice, witnesses for Allah, even against yourselves
Do not let the hatred of a people prevent you from being just. Be just; that is nearer to righteousness
Allah orders justice and good conduct
Make settlement between them in justice and act justly
We sent Our messengers with clear proofs, and sent down the Scripture and the balance that people may maintain justice

themes.description: Allah's boundless mercy and the call to be merciful to creation

ayahs.key_phrase: Your Lord has decreed upon Himself mercy
My mercy encompasses all things
We have not sent you except as a mercy to the worlds
Do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

themes.description: Establishing prayer as the pillar of faith and connection to Allah

ayahs.key_phrase: Establish prayer and give zakah and bow with those who bow
Establish prayer. Indeed, prayer prohibits immorality and wrongdoing
Establish prayer for My remembrance
Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers a decree of specified times
Recite what is easy from the Quran

themes.description: Spending in the way of Allah — zakah, sadaqah, and helping those in need

ayahs.key_phrase: The example of those who spend in the way of Allah is like a seed which grows seven ears
Spend from the good things which you have earned
The men who give charity and the women who give charity — it will be multiplied for them
Zakah is for the poor, the needy, those employed to collect it...
Those who spend their wealth by night and by day, secretly and publicly

themes.description: Seeking forgiveness from Allah and returning to the straight path

ayahs.key_phrase: Say: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah
Turn to Allah with sincere repentance
Do they not know that Allah accepts repentance from His servants?
Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work — Allah will replace their evil deeds with good
Seek forgiveness of your Lord and repent to Him

themes.description: The inevitable Day of Reckoning when all souls will be held accountable

ayahs.key_phrase: Whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it
When the sky breaks apart
When the Event occurs
The Striking Calamity — what is the Striking Calamity?
Indeed, the Day of Judgment is an appointed time

themes.description: The eternal reward for the righteous — gardens beneath which rivers flow

ayahs.key_phrase: But for he who has feared the standing before his Lord are two gardens
And the forerunners — they are the ones brought near, in Gardens of Pleasure
Whoever is removed from the Fire and admitted to Paradise has attained success
Gardens of perpetual residence beneath which rivers flow
He rewarded them for their patience with a garden and silk

themes.description: The punishment prepared for the disbelievers and the unjust

ayahs.key_phrase: Fear the Fire, whose fuel is people and stones, prepared for the disbelievers
He will surely be thrown into the Crusher
What put you into Saqar?
When they are thrown into it, they hear from it a dreadful inhaling
Indeed, the tree of Zaqqum is food for the sinful

themes.description: The nature, pillars, and fruits of true belief in Allah and the unseen

ayahs.key_phrase: Who believe in the unseen, establish prayer, and spend out of what We have provided
The bedouins say 'We believe.' Say: You have not yet believed; rather say 'We have submitted'
The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts become fearful
Successful indeed are the believers
The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and so have the believers

themes.description: The dangers of hypocrisy — those who claim faith but harbor disbelief

ayahs.key_phrase: Among people are those who say 'We believe' but they are not believers
When the hypocrites come to you they say 'We testify that you are the Messenger of Allah' — and Allah knows that they are liars
Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire
The hypocrite men and hypocrite women are of one another. They enjoin evil and forbid what is right
When they meet those who believe, they say 'We believe'; but when alone with their devils, they say 'We are with you'

themes.description: Rights and responsibilities within family — marriage, parenting, and kinship

ayahs.key_phrase: He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them, and He placed between you affection and mercy
O mankind, fear your Lord, who created you from one soul and created from it its mate
We have enjoined upon man care for his parents — his mother carried him in weakness upon weakness
Do not say to them 'uff' and do not repel them but speak to them a noble word
Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire

themes.description: Unity of the Muslim ummah, mutual support, and social responsibility

ayahs.key_phrase: The believers are but brothers, so make settlement between your brothers
Hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and do not become divided
We made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. The most noble among you is the most righteous
You are the best nation produced for mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong
Those who conduct their affairs by mutual consultation

themes.description: Allah's creation of the heavens, earth, humanity, and all living things

ayahs.key_phrase: The heavens and the earth were a joined entity, and We separated them and made from water every living thing
And of all things We created two mates; perhaps you will remember
We created man from an extract of clay
He who created seven heavens in layers. You do not see any inconsistency in the creation of the Most Merciful
Created man from a clinging clot

themes.description: The pursuit of knowledge as an act of worship and a path to understanding Allah's signs

ayahs.key_phrase: Read in the name of your Lord who created
Say: My Lord, increase me in knowledge
Allah will raise those who have believed among you and those given knowledge by degrees
Are those who know equal to those who do not know?
Only those fear Allah, from among His servants, who have knowledge

themes.description: Guarding the tongue, speaking truth, avoiding backbiting, slander, and vain talk

ayahs.key_phrase: Avoid much suspicion... and do not spy or backbite each other
Woe to every slanderer and backbiter
O you who believe, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice
And they who turn away from ill speech
Do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge. Indeed, hearing, sight, and the heart will all be questioned

themes.description: The nature of worldly wealth as a test — neither hoarding nor excess

ayahs.key_phrase: Know that the life of this world is but amusement and diversion and adornment
Competition in worldly increase diverts you
Wealth and children are the adornment of worldly life, but enduring good deeds are better
Those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend it in the way of Allah — give them tidings of a painful punishment
Seek the home of the Hereafter through what Allah has given you, and do not forget your share of the world

themes.description: Walking humbly on the earth, avoiding arrogance and pride

ayahs.key_phrase: The servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth in humility
Do not turn your cheek in contempt toward people and do not walk through the earth exultantly
Do not walk upon the earth exultantly. You will never tear the earth apart or reach the mountains in height
Worship Allah and associate nothing with Him, and to parents do good
It is not for you to be arrogant therein — so get out; indeed, you are of the debased

themes.description: Allah's forgiveness and the virtue of forgiving others

ayahs.key_phrase: The retribution for an evil act is one like it, but whoever pardons and makes reconciliation — his reward is from Allah
Who restrain anger and who pardon the people — and Allah loves the doers of good
Let them pardon and overlook. Would you not like that Allah should forgive you?
Whoever is patient and forgives — indeed, that is of the matters requiring resolve
Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred

themes.description: Fulfilling covenants with Allah and with people — the sanctity of promises

ayahs.key_phrase: O you who believe, fulfill your contracts
Fulfill My covenant, and I will fulfill your covenant
And fulfill every commitment. Indeed, the commitment is ever questioned about
Fulfill the covenant of Allah when you have taken it, and do not break oaths after their confirmation
Those who fulfill the covenant of Allah and do not break the contract

themes.description: Natural phenomena as signs pointing to Allah's existence and power

ayahs.key_phrase: Your Lord inspired to the bee: Take for yourself among the mountains, houses
In the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of night and day are signs
Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your languages and colors
Do you not see that Allah drives clouds? Then He brings them together, then makes them into a mass
Exalted is He who created all pairs — from what the earth grows and from themselves and from that which they do not know

themes.description: Lessons from the lives of prophets sent by Allah throughout history

ayahs.key_phrase: In their stories is a lesson for those of understanding
We sent not before you any messenger except that We revealed to him: There is no deity except Me, so worship Me
And We gave him Isaac and Jacob — all of them We guided. And Noah, We guided before
We believe in Allah and what has been revealed to us and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob
We have already sent messengers before you. Among them are those We have related to you, and among them are those We have not

themes.description: Rules and ethics of fighting in self-defense — boundaries, proportionality, and peace

ayahs.key_phrase: Fight in the way of Allah those who fight you but do not transgress. Indeed, Allah does not like transgressors
If they incline to peace, then incline to it also and rely upon Allah
Permission to fight has been given to those who are being fought, because they were wronged
Allah does not forbid you from being righteous and just toward those who do not fight you
Fighting has been enjoined upon you while it is hateful to you. Perhaps you hate a thing and it is good for you

themes.description: The honor, rights, and roles of women in Islam — inheritance, dignity, and spiritual equality

ayahs.key_phrase: For men is a share of what the parents and close relatives leave, and for women is a share
Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women... for them Allah has prepared forgiveness and a great reward
Live with them in kindness
The believing men and believing women are allies of one another
I do not allow the work of any worker among you to be lost, whether male or female

themes.description: Protecting the rights of orphans and treating them with kindness and justice

ayahs.key_phrase: As for the orphan, do not oppress him
Give to the orphans their properties and do not substitute the defective for the good
They ask you about orphans. Say: Improvement for them is best
That is the one who drives away the orphan
Those who consume the property of orphans unjustly are only consuming fire into their bellies

themes.description: Honesty in business dealings, prohibition of usury, and fair measurement

ayahs.key_phrase: Allah has permitted trade and has forbidden interest (riba)
Woe to those who give less than due — who, when they take a measure from people, take in full
Do not consume one another's wealth unjustly but only in lawful business by mutual consent
Give full measure and weight and do not deprive people of their due
When you contract a debt for a specified term, write it down

themes.description: Calling upon Allah in prayer, need, and gratitude — the essence of worship

ayahs.key_phrase: I am near. I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me
Call upon Me; I will respond to you
Say: What would my Lord care for you if not for your supplication?
Zakariya called upon his Lord: My Lord, grant me from Yourself good offspring
My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and from my descendants. Our Lord, accept my supplication

themes.description: Keeping Allah in mind and heart at all times — the tranquility of remembrance

ayahs.key_phrase: Verily, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest
Remember Me, I will remember you
O you who believe, remember Allah with much remembrance
And remember your Lord when you forget
The remembrance of Allah is greater

themes.description: Placing complete reliance on Allah while taking the means available

ayahs.key_phrase: When you have decided, then rely upon Allah. Indeed, Allah loves those who rely upon Him
Whoever relies upon Allah — then He is sufficient for him
And upon Allah let the believers rely
Whoever relies upon Allah — then indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise
Judgment is only for Allah. Upon Him I have relied, and upon Him let those who would rely, rely

themes.description: Life as a test from Allah — trials of wealth, health, fear, and loss

ayahs.key_phrase: We will surely test you with something of fear, hunger, loss of wealth, lives, and fruits
He who created death and life to test you — which of you is best in deed
Do the people think that they will be left to say 'We believe' and they will not be tried?
Every soul will taste death. And We test you with evil and with good as trial
Indeed, We have made that which is on the earth adornment for it that We may test them

themes.description: The promise of immense reward for righteous actions, even the smallest

ayahs.key_phrase: Whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it
Indeed, the patient will be given their reward without account
Whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while being a believer — We will surely cause them to live a good life
Indeed, We will not allow to be lost the reward of any who did well in deeds
Those who believe and do righteous deeds — We will surely remove from them their misdeeds

themes.description: Allah's perfect wisdom in all affairs — nothing happens without His knowledge and will

ayahs.key_phrase: Perhaps you hate a thing and it is good for you; and perhaps you love a thing and it is bad for you. And Allah knows while you do not
Allah has already set for everything a decreed extent
Indeed, all things We created with predestination
Say: Never will we be struck except by what Allah has decreed for us
No disaster strikes upon the earth or among yourselves except that it is in a register before We bring it into being
